catalog-cam-20-2-reading-part-1-q1.explanation:
Xác định heading “Appearance”
Tìm đoạn mô tả sự khác nhau giữa manatee và dugong
Chỉ có “tail” được nhắc tới về hình dạng khác nhau

catalog-cam-20-2-reading-part-1-q2.explanation:
Keyword: turn bodies around / look sideways
Tìm câu chứa “left or right”
“steering with its flippers” = dùng flippers để điều hướng cơ thể

catalog-cam-20-2-reading-part-1-q3.explanation:
Keyword: sense vibrations
Từ đồng nghĩa:
by means of = using
Danh từ cần điền là “hairs”

catalog-cam-20-2-reading-part-1-q4.explanation:
aquatic vegetation = marine plants
such as → cần ví dụ cụ thể
Ví dụ đầu tiên được nêu là seagrasses

catalog-cam-20-2-reading-part-1-q5.explanation:
Keyword: grasp and pull up plants
Chức năng “grasp” thuộc về lips

catalog-cam-20-2-reading-part-1-q6.explanation:
regulate = adjust
bodies floating ability = buoyancy

catalog-cam-20-2-reading-part-1-q7.explanation:
Câu:
“West Indian manatees can be found in a variety of different aquatic habitats.”

Đoạn:
“It moves between fresh water and salt water, taking advantage of coastal mangroves and coral reefs, rivers, lakes and inland lagoons.”

Có rất nhiều môi trường sống khác nhau.

=> TRUE

catalog-cam-20-2-reading-part-1-q8.explanation:
Câu:
“The Florida manatee lives in warmer waters than the Antillean manatee.”

Bài chỉ nói:

Florida manatee ở Florida vào mùa đông
cần nước ấm khi nhiệt độ dưới 20°C

Không có sự so sánh trực tiếp nhiệt độ nước giữa Florida và Antillean manatee.

=> NOT GIVEN

Bẫy:
Có nhắc “warm-water outfalls” nhưng không hề nói Florida sống ở vùng ấm hơn Antillean.

catalog-cam-20-2-reading-part-1-q9.explanation:
Câu:
“African manatee’s range is limited to coastal waters…”

Đoạn:
“The species also makes use of rivers, with the mammals seen in landlocked countries such as Mali and Niger.”

Không chỉ ở coastal waters mà còn sống trong sông và quốc gia không giáp biển.

=> FALSE

catalog-cam-20-2-reading-part-1-q10.explanation:
Câu:
“The extent of the loss ... was only revealed many years later.”

Đoạn chỉ nói:
“It is estimated that 140,000 Amazonian manatees were killed between 1935 and 1954”

Không có thông tin:

khi nào người ta phát hiện ra số liệu này
có phải nhiều năm sau mới biết hay không

=> NOT GIVEN

catalog-cam-20-2-reading-part-1-q11.explanation:
Đoạn:
“Both are also expected to undergo a decline of 20% over the next 40 years.”

Câu hỏi:
“populations will fall in the coming decades”

expected decline = predicted fall
next 40 years = coming decades

=> TRUE

catalog-cam-20-2-reading-part-1-q12.explanation:
Câu:
“The risk ... increased significantly in the period 2009-2020.”

Đoạn:
“A review ... from 2009 to 2020 found that at least 700 cases involved manatees.”

Chỉ cho số liệu tổng hợp.
Không có thông tin:

risk increased
increased significantly

=> NOT GIVEN

Bẫy:
Có mốc thời gian nhưng không có xu hướng tăng.

catalog-cam-20-2-reading-part-1-q13.explanation:
Statement:
“There is some legislation ... to reduce the likelihood of boat strikes.”

Đoạn:
“laws in certain parts of Florida now limit boat speeds during winter”

Mục đích:
“allowing slow-moving manatees more time to respond.”

Bước 1: Paraphrase

laws = legislation
reduce likelihood of boat strikes = give them more time to avoid collisions

catalog-cam-20-2-reading-part-2-q14.explanation:
Câu hỏi:
“mention of false assumptions about why people procrastinate”

Keyword:

false assumptions
why people procrastinate

Đoạn B mở đầu:
“Contrary to popular belief, procrastination is not due to laziness or poor time management.”

Phân tích:

contrary to popular belief = trái với suy nghĩ phổ biến
tức là đang nói về những assumption sai.

catalog-cam-20-2-reading-part-2-q15.explanation:
Câu hỏi:
“reference to the realisation that others also procrastinate”

Keyword:

others also procrastinate

Đoạn F:
“We should remind ourselves that we’re not the first person to procrastinate, nor the last.”

Phân tích:
Tác giả khuyên nên nhận ra rằng:

mình không phải người duy nhất procrastinate.

→ exactly match câu hỏi.

→ Đáp án F.

catalog-cam-20-2-reading-part-2-q16.explanation:
Câu hỏi:
“neurological evidence of a link between procrastination and emotion”

Keyword:

neurological evidence
emotion

Đoạn B:
“Research involving brain imaging has found that areas of the brain linked to detection of threats and emotion regulation are actually different...”

Phân tích:

brain imaging = bằng chứng thần kinh học
emotion regulation = điều tiết cảm xúc

→ Đáp án B.

catalog-cam-20-2-reading-part-2-q17.explanation:
Câu:
“Many people think that procrastination is the result of ___.”

Bước 1: Tìm keyword

result of
many people think

Đoạn B:
“Contrary to popular belief, procrastination is not due to laziness or poor time management.”

Phân tích:
Summary đang paraphrase:

due to = result of
popular belief = many people think

→ Đáp án: laziness.

Bẫy:
Có “poor time management” phía sau nhưng câu sau summary đã nói tiếp:
“Others believe... inability to organise time efficiently.”

catalog-cam-20-2-reading-part-2-q18.explanation:
Câu:
“cause us to feel ___ when we think about them”

Đoạn B:
“If just thinking about the task threatens our sense of self-worth or makes us anxious...”

Phân tích:

makes us anxious = cause us to feel anxious

→ Đáp án: anxious.

catalog-cam-20-2-reading-part-2-q19.explanation:
threats

Câu:
“brain regions associated with regulating emotions and identifying ___”

Đoạn:
“areas of the brain linked to detection of threats and emotion regulation”

Paraphrase:

emotion regulation = regulating emotions
detection = identifying

→ thứ được identify là threats.

→ Đáp án: threats.

catalog-cam-20-2-reading-part-2-q20.explanation:
Câu:
“Getting ready to take ___ might be a typical example”

Đoạn C:
“Tasks that are emotionally loaded or difficult, such as preparing for exams...”

Phân tích:

getting ready to take = preparing for
typical example = such as

→ Đáp án: exams.

catalog-cam-20-2-reading-part-2-q21.explanation:
Câu:
“People who are likely to procrastinate tend to be either ___ or those with low self-esteem.”

Đoạn C:
“People with low self-esteem are more likely to procrastinate. Another group ... are perfectionists”

Phân tích:
Summary đảo vị trí thông tin:

perfectionists
people with low self-esteem

→ Đáp án: perfectionists

catalog-cam-20-2-reading-part-2-q22.explanation:
Câu:
“It’s often followed by a feeling of ___”

Đoạn C:
“Afterwards, people tend to be left with a sense of guilt...”

→ feeling of = sense of

→ Đáp án: guilt.

catalog-cam-20-2-reading-part-2-q23.explanation:
Đề hỏi:
“Which TWO comparisons ... are mentioned?”

Option A:
“Their salaries are lower.”

Đoạn D:
“participants who said they regularly procrastinated had less annual income”

→ less annual income = salaries are lower

→ Đúng.

Option B:
“The quality of their work is inferior.”

Bài KHÔNG nói chất lượng công việc tệ hơn.

→ Sai.

Option C:
“They don’t keep their jobs for as long.”

Đoạn D:
“less employment stability”

→ việc làm không ổn định = khó giữ việc lâu dài

→ Đúng.

Option D:
“They don’t enjoy their working lives as much.”

Không có thông tin enjoyment.

→ Sai.

Option E:
“They have poorer relationships with colleagues.”

Không đề cập colleague relationships.

→ Sai.

→ Đáp án:
A + C.

catalog-cam-20-2-reading-part-2-q24.explanation:
Đề hỏi:
“Which TWO comparisons ... are mentioned?”

Option A:
“Their salaries are lower.”

Đoạn D:
“participants who said they regularly procrastinated had less annual income”

→ less annual income = salaries are lower

→ Đúng.

Option B:
“The quality of their work is inferior.”

Bài KHÔNG nói chất lượng công việc tệ hơn.

→ Sai.

Option C:
“They don’t keep their jobs for as long.”

Đoạn D:
“less employment stability”

→ việc làm không ổn định = khó giữ việc lâu dài

→ Đúng.

Option D:
“They don’t enjoy their working lives as much.”

Không có thông tin enjoyment.

→ Sai.

Option E:
“They have poorer relationships with colleagues.”

Không đề cập colleague relationships.

→ Sai.

→ Đáp án:
A + C.

catalog-cam-20-2-reading-part-2-q25.explanation:
Option A:
“not judging ourselves harshly”

Đoạn F:
“We should admit that we feel bad, but not be overly critical of ourselves.”

→ exactly match.

→ Đúng.

Option B:
“setting ourselves manageable aims”

Không có thông tin này.

→ Sai.

Option C:
“rewarding ourselves for tasks achieved”

Không đề cập self-reward.

→ Sai.

Option D:
“prioritising tasks according to importance”

Bài chỉ nói reminding ourselves why task is important, không phải prioritising.

→ Sai.

Option E:
“avoiding things that stop us concentrating on our tasks”

Đoạn F:
“strategies that can help us fend off distractions”

fend off distractions = tránh thứ làm mất tập trung

→ Đúng.

→ Đáp án:
A + E.

catalog-cam-20-2-reading-part-2-q26.explanation:
Option A:
“not judging ourselves harshly”

Đoạn F:
“We should admit that we feel bad, but not be overly critical of ourselves.”

→ exactly match.

→ Đúng.

Option B:
“setting ourselves manageable aims”

Không có thông tin này.

→ Sai.

Option C:
“rewarding ourselves for tasks achieved”

Không đề cập self-reward.

→ Sai.

Option D:
“prioritising tasks according to importance”

Bài chỉ nói reminding ourselves why task is important, không phải prioritising.

→ Sai.

Option E:
“avoiding things that stop us concentrating on our tasks”

Đoạn F:
“strategies that can help us fend off distractions”

fend off distractions = tránh thứ làm mất tập trung

→ Đúng.

→ Đáp án:
A + E.

catalog-cam-20-2-reading-part-3-q27.explanation:
Statement:
“When DeJesus first used ABS, he shared decision-making about strikes with it.”

Đoạn đầu:
“Instead of making any judgments himself about a strike, DeJesus had decisions fed to him through an earpiece”

Phân tích:

instead of = thay vì
tức là DeJesus KHÔNG cùng đưa ra quyết định với ABS.
ABS quyết định hoàn toàn.

→ Statement sai.

→ NO.

catalog-cam-20-2-reading-part-3-q28.explanation:
Statement:
“MLB considered it necessary to amend the size of the strike zone when criticisms were received from players.”

Đoạn:
“players complained about some strange calls. In response, MLB decided to tweak the dimensions of the zone”

Phân tích:

complained = criticisms
tweak the dimensions = amend the size

→ Ý khớp hoàn toàn.

→ YES.

catalog-cam-20-2-reading-part-3-q29.explanation:
Statement:
“MLB is keen to justify the money spent on improving the accuracy of ABS’s calculations.”

Bài có nói:

MLB says device is near-perfect
precise within fractions of an inch

NHƯNG:
Không có thông tin:

tiền bạc
justify spending
improving calculations

→ Statement tự thêm ý.

→ NOT GIVEN.

catalog-cam-20-2-reading-part-3-q30.explanation:
Statement:
“The hundred-mile-an-hour fastball led to a more exciting style of play.”

Đoạn:
“This type of baseball essentially hasn’t existed since the 1960s, when the hundred-mile-an-hour fastball ... entered the game. It flattened the game into strikeouts, walks, and home runs-a type of play lacking much action.”

Phân tích:

lacking much action = ít hấp dẫn hơn
flatten the game = làm game đơn điệu

→ hoàn toàn ngược với “more exciting”.

→ NO.

catalog-cam-20-2-reading-part-3-q31.explanation:
Statement:
“The differing proposals for alterations to the baseball bat led to fierce debate on Sword’s team.”

Đoạn:
“Sword’s team brainstormed potential fixes.”

“they talked about changing—from changing the bats to changing the geometry of the field.”

Không có:

fierce debate
tranh cãi dữ dội

→ NOT GIVEN.

catalog-cam-20-2-reading-part-3-q32.explanation:
Statement:
“ABS makes changes to the shape of the strike zone feasible.”

Đoạn:
“you can load any strike zone you want into the system.”

“It might be a triangle, or a blob...”

Phân tích:
ABS cho phép thay đổi shape của strike zone.

→ feasible = possible.

→ YES.

catalog-cam-20-2-reading-part-3-q33.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
MLB
human umpires
shout out decisions
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

MLB wanted human umpires to announce the calls, just as they would have done in the past.

Bước 3: Phân tích

"in the past" được paraphrase thành former roles (vai trò trước đây).

Các trọng tài vẫn thông báo kết quả giống như khi họ còn làm nhiệm vụ truyền thống.

→ former roles

catalog-cam-20-2-reading-part-3-q34.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
required
whether a ball was a strike
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

calling a strike was a judgment call on the part of the umpire.

Bước 3: Phân tích

"judgment call" = quyết định dựa trên nhận định cá nhân.

Paraphrase gần nhất là:

subjective assessment

→  subjective assessment

catalog-cam-20-2-reading-part-3-q35.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
strike
crosses through
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

a pitch that passes through the strike zone (an imaginary zone...)

Bước 3: Phân tích

Strike zone là vùng tưởng tượng.

"imaginary zone" ≈ "perceived area"

Không phải pitch boundary vì bài không mô tả ranh giới của cú ném mà mô tả một vùng không gian.
→ perceived area

catalog-cam-20-2-reading-part-3-q36.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
in the past
strike calls
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

countless arguments between a team's manager and the umpire
Bước 3: Phân tích
countless arguments
↓
many arguments
↓
numerous disputes
Đồng nghĩa trực tiếp.
→ numerous disputes

catalog-cam-20-2-reading-part-3-q37.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
first game
ABS
strike calls
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

there was no heckling and no shouted disagreement.
Nobody said a word.
Bước 3: Phân tích
Không ai phản ứng gì.
"Nobody said a word"
↓
"total silence"
→  total silence

catalog-cam-20-2-reading-part-3-q38.explanation:
Question:
“What does the writer suggest about ABS in the fifth paragraph?”

Đoạn 5 nói:

ABS too precise
unforgiving
legalistic
mất “dialogue” giữa pitcher và umpire

Ý tác giả:
ABS có thể làm mất yếu tố con người → giảm sức hấp dẫn.

→ Đáp án:
“It may reduce some of the appeal of the game.”

→ B.

catalog-cam-20-2-reading-part-3-q39.explanation:
Question:
Morgan Sword says ABS was introduced because...

Đoạn:
“executives are terrified of losing younger fans”

→ mục tiêu là giữ audience trẻ.

→ Đáp án:
“was an attempt to ensure baseball retained a young audience.”

→ D.

catalog-cam-20-2-reading-part-3-q40.explanation:
Question:
Why include views of Noë and Russo?

Cả hai đều nói:

perfection không phải điều tốt
công nghệ quá chính xác làm game mất tự nhiên
enjoyment quan trọng hơn absolute accuracy

Russo:
“What is there to talk about?”

→ Nếu mọi thứ perfect thì game mất vui.

→ Đáp án:
“to indicate that accuracy is not the same thing as enjoyment”

→ C.
